channel.title: RADAR — Student media feed (English)
channel.description: Quebec student newspaper headlines and briefs in English, aggregated by RADAR.

item.title: Ask the Trib: How do I survive summer in my hometown?
item.description:
Since returning to my hometown from McGill a little over a month ago, I’ve been so extremely bored. I’m out of errands to run—other than bumping into someone from my high school every time I leave my house. If I have to have the, “How was your year?” small talk conversation one more time, I might actually explode. Please please please, if you have any tips on how to survive life in my hometown for the next three grueling months of summer, share them. I’ll take anything.

By Brooke Barak — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Kanga I, II, III’ brings Projections: SANKOFA’s afrofuturist vision to Trottier
item.description:
Tanzanian-American-Canadian artist Shanna Strauss ’s screenprint triad Kanga I, II, III joined the student-led digital art exhibition Projections: SANKOFA on Feb. 23. Displayed in the Lorne M. Trottier Building mezzanine, the Kanga series combines negative space with vibrant colour to assert the unifying power of shared cultural identity.

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: AI’s use in legal decisions poses major risks to the Canadian judicial system’s integrity and neutrality
item.description:
On April 22, the Superior Court of Quebec annulled the August 2025 arbitration Association des Ressources Intermédiaires D’Hébergement du Québec (ARIHQ) v. Santé Québec , as the ruling was based on nonexistent, likely artificial intelligence ( AI )-generated legal doctrine and jurisprudence. Each citation of a legal precedent linked in the award led to completely different judgments ; the precedents did not actually exist.

By Nello Giuliani — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Know Your Artist: Theodore A. Harris
item.description:
American artist and poet Theodore A. Harris is a genius of the political collage . Born in Manhattan, New York and raised in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Harris brings an autobiographical focus to the subversive , polysemous medium of the collage, one shaped by his upbringing, mentors, and personal experiences with anti-Black discrimination and American capitalism’s exploitative nature.

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Montreal protestors commemorate the 78th anniversary of the Nakba
item.description:
On May 16, hundreds of protestors gathered in front of the Consulate General of Israel in Montreal to commemorate the 78th anniversary of the Nakba ( Nakba Day ). Demonstrators waved Palestinian, Rotisken’rakéhte ( Mohawk Warrior Society ), and post-1979 revolution Iranian flags amidst the music of live drummers.

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: A final note from the Editor-in-Chief of Volume 45, for the record
item.description:
One week before The Tribune ’s first production night, I found myself in Toronto in a black dress, black shoes, and sparkles on my eyelids. I was at the funeral of Fabienne de Cartier, a former News Editor.

By Yusur Al-Sharqi — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: SPHR remembers Gaza Solidarity Encampment ahead of Nakba Anniversary Protest
item.description:
On May 9, Students for Palestine’s Honour and Resistance ( SPHR ) at McGill hosted a gathering to commemorate the two-year anniversary of Montreal community members establishing the Gaza Solidarity Encampment on McGill’s Lower Field. 743 days later, community members of all ages met for a potluck, arts-and-crafts, and a letter-writing campaign for Palestinian political prisoners, intended to raise awareness of, and inspire solidarity against, Israel’s prison conditions.

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Last day to apply to be part of The Concordian's masthead!
item.description:
May 2 at 11:59 p.m. is the deadline to apply to be on The Concordian's Volume 44 masthead

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: McGill community members express concerns about McGill Athletics’ former partnership with InterRent REIT
item.description:
Across the 18 months she has lived in her Plateau apartment, Alice Lee, U3 Arts & Science, has had her water shut off 13 times. Most recently—in February 2026—she turned on her tap to find the water running yellow, as she recounted in an interview with The Tribune . Lee

By Rebecca Hamilton — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Journalism After Gaza’ panel discusses media bias and the future of reporting in Palestine
item.description:
On April 9, the McGill Institute of Islamic Studies hosted a panel event titled “ Journalism After Gaza: A Roundtable Discussion on the Present & Future of the Media ,” featuring Saman Malik , Sana Saeed , and Kareem Shaheen . The panel was led by Pasha M. Khan , associate

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The Concordian is hiring!
item.description:
Masthead positions at The Concordian are now open for the 2026-27 academic year

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

item.title: Apply to The Concordian Awards!
item.description:
Have you contributed to Volume 43 of The Concordian?

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The Honey Bank: ASFA, Austerity, and Student Mobilization
item.description:
Listen now | In this episode of the Honey Bank, Adam Semergian, academic coordinator of the Arts and Science Federation of Associations (ASFA) talks about student mobilization against austerity measures at Concordia, specifically the recent special general meeting hosted by ASFA.

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Sad endings do not ruin a great story: Haliburton, Vanmoen, and Carlyle
item.description:
Sad endings are unfortunately part of life, but they do not have to ruin our greatest stories. The most popular romance movie on Letterboxd is La La Land , a movie with a horribly unsatisfying ending to an otherwise lovely story. That summary is one so often befitting in sports.

By Will Kennedy — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: How can I show you I’m doing better if there’s nothing good for me to do?
item.description:
Canada’s prison system is predicated on rehabilitation and reintegration—yet, the country’s own correctional service is defunding the most integral program to realizing that aim. Correctional Service Canada (CSC) has announced the suspension of its federal prison education

By Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The Seagull: An Automorphic Play About Art and Humanity
item.description:
A review of the Strand of the Night Theatre’s production at Moyse Hall with director and cast interviews The post The Seagull: An Automorphic Play About Art and Humanity appeared first on The McGill Daily .

By Sonia Berman — Via The McGill Daily (McGill)

Photo: Janna Toubal

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: This Week's Top Stories
item.description:
Check out our final issue on stands and online now!

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Your health, your problem
item.description:
“I got in an accident that involved some sea urchins,” Natalie Joy Gale, a PhD student in McGill’s Department of History and Classical Studies, said in an interview with //The Tribune//. Gale recalls wiping out in an ‘urchin-y’ area of reef while surfing in Puerto Rico

By Leanne Cherry — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Oh, the places I’ll go (but not stay)!
item.description:
At the airport, I learned to expect the pauses, the extra questions, the glances at my passport that lingered a second too long. I did everything right—got into McGill, applied for my visa, and carried proof that I would leave once my degree was over. Even in places I call home

By Rupneet Shahriar — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Financing My Future: Life Without Financial Worries
item.description:
In episode 9 of Financing My Future , Christelle shares how her stable, supported reality contrasts sharply with the typical struggling student narrative. A 20-year-old journalism student at Concordia and The Concordian’ s Podcast Editor, she’s grown up with her needs fully

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Open Letter: How can I show you I’m doing better if there’s nothing good for me to do?
item.description:
The Tribune ‘s special issue for the Winter 2026 semester was centred around the theme of ‘ memory ,’ with our writers, staff, editors, and creative team discussing the role of institutional, collective, and personal memory in society, politics, educational institutions, and

By Student at the Cowansville penitentiary — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Black Sisterhood at McGill targeted with online racist harassment
item.description:
Content warning: Mentions of racial violence After Black Sisterhood at McGill (BSISSY) began recruiting members to start an Alpha Kappa Alpha (AKA) sorority chapter at McGill, co-founders Lena Karis Moussio, U1 Arts, and Astou Badiane, U1 Arts, received racist comments and

By Moyọ Alabi and Ellen Lurie — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Referendum to boycott Israeli institutions passes with the highest voting turnout in recent LSA history
item.description:
On March 19, voting for the Referendum Regarding the Academic and Cultural Boycott of Israel to Preserve Academic Freedom (PACBI) opened to all members of the Law Students’ Association ( LSA ). The motion, put forward by Law Students for Palestine at McGill ( LS4PM ) and McGill

By Amelia H. Clark — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The forgotten history of the Montreal coffeehouse
item.description:
Before the price of coffee skyrocketed and the death of third spaces ; before the tyrannical reign of Ticketmaster and the monopolization of the music industry ; before we slipped into antisocial seclusion and let blue light mollify our beautiful brains , there was once an

By Kendyl Daley — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: A reflection on McGill’s science programs from graduating SciTech staff writers
item.description:
If you’re into bio, you’d better learn to love DNA . Breathe it. Dream about it. Because everything comes back to DNA. What’s the reason behind ‘phenomenon X?’ A gene. ‘Phenomenon Y?’ Another gene. Are you curious about the composition of a microbial community? Sequence another

By The editorial team — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: McGill investigating antisemitic vandalism in Faculty of Medicine
item.description:
Content warning: Antisemitism and violent threats A recent act of antisemitic vandalism at McGill is raising renewed concerns about campus safety for Jewish students. The graffiti, found in a bathroom stall in the Faculty of Medicine, read “Kill all Jews” and “Jews out of McGill

By Yusur Al-Sharqi — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Senior spotlight: Sophie Courville, Ayoub Sabri, and Erik Linseisen
item.description:
Sophie Courville Sophie Courville, a physiology senior and Cross Country runner, was voted Réseau du sport étudiant du Québec (RSEQ) Women’s Cross Country Athlete of the Year, led the team with a fifth-place finish at the U SPORTS National Championships, and earned all-star

By Clara Smyrski and Ivanna Zhang — Via The Tribune (McGill)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Find 5 Gaiters in this photo!
item.description:
Answer Key Below

By The editorial team — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

item.title: From the rink to the links
item.description:
Henri Dessureaux – Contributor Recently Gabrielle Santerre won her third straight female athlete of the year award, to add to her U-Sports MVP, playoff MVP and U-Sports championship amongst other awards. Her accolades on the ice are outstanding, having notched 99 points in 67

By Henri Dessureaux — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Jo Aubert: The testimony of a graduating athlete
item.description:
As the school year finally comes to a wrap, I wanted to interview an athlete from the school to get insight on their thoughts and feelings about their time here. For the interview I went down a different path when choosing an athlete, opting to interview a club athlete instead

By The editorial team — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Ringette: a club deep-dive and insight into next season
item.description:
Shelly-Ann Canares – Contributor You’ve heard about the hockey club and the figure skating club, but what other Bishop’s club is played on ice? First created last winter by two dedicated players from Quebec and B.C., Shelly and Abigale decided it was time to bring their

By Shelly-Ann Canares — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Cheers, tears and a Gaiter reveal?
item.description:
After a hard fought year, Gaiters Athletics has come to a close. Despite the numerous ups and downs this year brought, Thursday, Apr. 2 saw everyone come together and celebrate the 2025-26 athletic year. The athletic banquet is a chance for one final send off before the

By Leighton Geraghty — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: A season to remember: BU Men’s Club Hockey honour a season built on commitment
item.description:
Owen Grand – Contributor The Bishop’s Men’s Club Hockey closed out a memorable season this past Saturday with their annual banquet, held at the Gait. The evening brought together players, coaches, supporters and alumni for a night to reflect on both the success of the season and

By Owen Grand — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The best thing you can do at Bishop’s is get involved
item.description:
When I first came to Bishop’s, I never could have imagined that in my last year, I would be attending the United Nations in New York City. On Mar. 9, I had the privilege of attending the 70th Commission on the Status of Women (CSW) as a delegate with the Canadian Federation of

By Olivia Woods — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Step outside… and change your life
item.description:
If you had the chance to finish high school 6,500 kilometres from home in a small Swiss town, would you do it? Spending my last year of high school living in Switzerland changed my life and I believe that living abroad, even for a short amount of time, is one of the best ways to

By Piper Howell — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Robin Williams lied (kind of)
item.description:
At the ripe age of 15, when you’ve finally figured out the right amount of deodorant to use and the consequences of putting water in your parents’ vodka, you are asked to choose your career path. Not just any path either, the one that will decide what seems to be your fate. So

By Francis Paquin — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: No one tells you when it’s over
item.description:
Jonny Roach- Staff Writer The page looks the same as it always does: blank, waiting, familiar. I’ve sat here before, more times than I can count, chasing a first sentence that never feels right until it suddenly does. It’s routine now, almost muscle memory. But this time

By Jonny Roach — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Photo: credit: The Campus

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Grad Formal 2026
item.description:
Emilie Vadeboncoeur – Director of Marketing and Communications As the end of another school year quickly approaches, the Bishop’s University Student Representative Council (BUSRC) is preparing to host one of the most anticipated events for graduating students, the 2026 Grad

By The editorial team — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: The purple circle
item.description:
Before arriving at Bishop’s University, Max Harrison had heard whispers about the institution’s strong alumni network, but its impact became clear during his first year, when a cold message he sent on LinkedIn received a response within minutes. Now a fourth-year Business

By Max Harrison — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: WSB Recognition Gala
item.description:
On Friday, Mar. 27, the Williams School of Business (WSB) hosted the second annual WSB Recognition Gala. Held at the Gait, this event brought together students, faculty, staff and partners to celebrate and recognize accomplishments across the Williams School of Business. The

By Chloë Beauvais — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Mars enters Aries
item.description:
Abigail Epstein – Layout Editor While Mars in Aries imparts action, Apr. 9, the last quarter moon cautions transition as the tune of its emotional tide, Apr. 10. Aries: You may feel conflicted, chasing the close of a cycle with sudden inspirations. Try to direct your drive

By The editorial team — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: THE NUMBERED? I HOPE THEY COUNTED CORRECTLY!
item.description:
Every person who entered Turner Studio’s theatre from Mar. 25 to 29 was given a necklace with a small locket on it and was welcomed to the world of “The Numbered.” A poster on the door to the theatre stated, “This is your locket. You’ve worn it since you were born. You cherish

By Tallula Russell — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Between light and emotion: Rhythm and Radiance’s final bow
item.description:
Romane Bessiere – Contributor On Mar. 21, the Bishop’s dancers set the stage on fire at the Centennial Theater with Rhythm and Radiance, the annual showcase of Bishop’s University, bringing together the competitive team and the club for an evening marked by energy, emotion and

By Romane Bessiere — Via The Campus (Bishop's University)

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Read It Up | The Castle of Otranto by Horace Walpole
item.description:

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

item.title: The Pitch List
item.description:
Welcome to The Concordian’s final Pitch List for Volume 43 ! If you’re interested in covering any of these topics, send an email to the respective Section Editor no later than Tuesday morning. Your first draft is due by Wednesday unless otherwise discussed with the Section

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

item.title: This Week's Top Stories
item.description:
Check out our new issue on stands and online now!

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

item.title: Financing My Future: In Full Financial Honesty
item.description:
Spending reflects identity — what we value, what we avoid, and what we believe we deserve. Numbers in bank accounts don’t determine happiness, yet finances can feel like a verdict of your worth. I felt this early.

By The editorial team — Via The Concordian (Concordia)

Photo: credit: The Concordian

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Spotlighting Powerful McGill Alumnae
item.description:
In honour of our International Women’s History special issue, The McGill Daily has highlighted six influential McGill alumnae who I have flourished in their respective fields.

By The editorial team — Via The McGill Daily (McGill)

Photo: Yunjie Zhang

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

item.title: Reporting in a Warzone
item.description:
On March 11, Professor Maria Popova coordinated a zoom call with Luca Léry Moffat, an economics reporter for The Kyiv Independent , for her Politics of East Central Europe class. The students convened to ask Moffat about his experiences working in Ukraine and his perspective on

By Eva Marriott-Fabre and Sena Ho — Via The McGill Daily (McGill)

Photo: Courtesy of The Kyiv Independent

Aggregated by RADAR — link opens the original student publication.

media:content.media:credit: Photo: Courtesy of The Kyiv Independent
